To solve this, we first need to understand what an amphibian is. 
Amphibians are cold-blooded animals that hatch from eggs in water. They are unique among animals because they spend at least part of their lives in water and part of their lives on land. They have permeable skin that allows them to exchange gases easily, which is why they need to stay in moist environments. Some amphibians also have a special sticky secretion that helps them cling to smooth surfaces.
